original_prompt: Perform the following tasks in the order they are given: 1. How many appointments do I have on May 15th, 2024? Please give me a summary of the description for each appointment. 2. Am I free for lunch at 12:00 on 2024-05-19? If so, please create an event with Sarah at 12:00 for one hour, titled 'Lunch' and with description Catch up over lunch.. Her email address is 'sarah.connor@gmail.com'.
assessment_reasoning: The prompt gives a clear two-step instruction: count and summarize appointments on 2024-05-15, then check availability at 12:00 on 2024-05-19 and, if free, create a one-hour event with specified title, description and attendee email. Key details (dates, time, title, description, attendee email, duration, and conditional creation) are explicit. Remaining minor ambiguities: timezone for the given times is not specified; which calendar(s) to check/use (default account, shared calendars, or all calendars) is not stated; how to handle recurring events or all-day events that overlap; and what to do or report if the user is not free at the requested time beyond not creating the event. The prompt is otherwise direct and actionable.

variations.L1: Do a couple things with my calendar: tell me how many appointments I have on May 15, 2024 and give a short summary for each. Then check if I'm free for lunch on May 19 at 12:00 — if I am, set up a lunch with Sarah (sarah.connor@gmail.com).
variations.L2: Check my calendar and answer two things: 1) How many appointments do I have on 2024-05-15? Give a brief summary of each. 2) Am I free at 12:00 on 2024-05-19? If so, please create a one-hour event at 12:00 with Sarah (email: sarah.connor@gmail.com).
variations.L3: Please perform the following in order: (1) For May 15, 2024, count how many appointments are on my calendar and provide a one-sentence summary of the description for each appointment. (2) For 2024-05-19, check whether I have anything at 12:00 (noon). If there are no overlapping events at that time, create a calendar event titled 'Lunch' from 12:00 to 13:00 with description 'Catch up over lunch.' and add Sarah (sarah.connor@gmail.com) as an attendee. If there is a conflict, report the conflicting event(s) instead of creating the event.
variations.L4: Perform the following tasks in the order they are given: 1. How many appointments do I have on May 15th, 2024? Please give me a summary of the description for each appointment. 2. Am I free for lunch at 12:00 on 2024-05-19? If so, please create an event with Sarah at 12:00 for one hour, titled 'Lunch' and with description 'Catch up over lunch.' Her email address is 'sarah.connor@gmail.com'.
variations.L5: Execute these steps in order using the calendar API (use UTC timezone unless I say otherwise). Step 1: Query all events on 2024-05-15 from 00:00:00Z to 23:59:59Z and return a JSON object with two fields: may15_count (integer) and may15_events (array of objects with keys id, start_iso, end_iso, title, description, attendees_emails). Step 2: Check availability on 2024-05-19 starting at 12:00:00Z for 60 minutes. Treat me as 'free' only if there are no events overlapping that exact interval with status 'busy' or 'confirmed'. If free, call create_event with arguments: title='Lunch', start='2024-05-19T12:00:00Z', end='2024-05-19T13:00:00Z', description='Catch up over lunch.', attendees=['sarah.connor@gmail.com'], send_invitations=true; then return the created event object. If not free, do not create an event and instead return the conflicting event(s). Return the complete result as a JSON object: {may15_count, may15_events, may19_availability:'free'|'busy', created_event:null|event_object, conflicts:[]}. Perform only these steps and follow the specified formats exactly.
